mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation within the next 20 trading days, lifting S&P 500 returns through a lower discount-rate channel and improved risk appetite.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS over any 3-year rolling window, or if the sign flips consistently in easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5%, tighter policy compresses liquidity and increases discount rates, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns turn negative after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns stays below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, causing S&P 500 returns to be negative over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly increases in UNRATE fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign remains positive in more than 60% of rising-UNRATE episodes over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to trigger earnings risk repricing, which makes S&P 500 forward 5-day returns negative.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not negative after the specified UNRATE acceleration threshold, or if the thresholded event does not outperform a random-date baseline in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth captured by RSAFS together with firm employment growth captured by PAYEMS indicates resilient final demand, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal portfolio based on RSAFS and PAYEMS does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either series alone over any 3-year rolling window, or if the interaction term is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO during an already high-UNRATE environment signals an economy-wide production slowdown, and that combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as margins and revenues weaken.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-down and UNRATE-high state does not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive value beyond either series individually.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below its recent level signals weakening housing demand and tighter cyclical activity, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained HOUST declines fail to precede weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in the majority of down-HOUST regimes over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving GDPC1 and rising RSAFS jointly confirm a rebound in aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the signal fails when GDP is flat or sales soften.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising GDPC1 and RSAFS do not show positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint effect disappears once either proxy is included alone.
